review_comment: This should test for generic labels such as `MyLabel<T>(PhantomData<T>)`. This is a use-case I do have.

I've added test cases for empty struct and tuple variants, both standalone and as part of an enum. I had to adjust `static_ref_impl` for it to work and stumbled across some weird behavior that is only present in the current stable channel, preventing me from using `&Self::EmptyTuple()` and having to use `&EnumLabel::EmptyTuple()` instead.  

On the Rust playground, [this](https://play.rust-lang.org/?version=stable&mode=release&edition=2021&gist=c544fa6b762881a53a46c8aae4e702a6) compiles, but [this](https://play.rust-lang.org/?version=stable&mode=release&edition=2021&gist=39468824e70ec79869792d83900d95f6) doesn't. Both versions work on the current beta channel.
You should be able to just do `&Self {}`, which would work for unit structs, as well as tuple and named structs with no fields.
Ah, you're right. That's very useful to know.
